pathwayTagline: Protect → observe → move → restore → clean → feed → eliminate
studentFacingPrinciple: Evaluators call skills by official name aloud — not by exam card number. Numbers are reference-only on the skill detail page.

sections.title: Infection Control
sections.rationale: Every clinical skill begins and ends with infection control. Students must internalize this as muscle memory before touching a patient.

sections.title: Measurement and Recording
sections.rationale: Observation-only skills with no physical intervention. They train precision and documentation before students are trusted to act.

sections.title: Mobility and Positioning
sections.rationale: Before personal care, students must move patients safely. Positioning and transfers prevent injury during bathing, dressing, and elimination.

sections.title: Restorative and Support
sections.rationale: Once mobility is established, students learn to maintain and restore function — preventing deterioration, not just assisting movement.

sections.title: Personal Care
sections.rationale: The core of daily CNA work. Mobility skills are prerequisites for bathing, grooming, and dressing with dignity.

sections.title: Feeding and Nutrition
sections.rationale: Feeding follows hygiene and positioning. It is cognitively distinct — swallowing observation, aspiration risk, and dignity at the table.

sections.title: Elimination and Peri Care
sections.rationale: The most intimate care. Full confidence in infection control, mobility, and personal care comes first — then elimination.

skills.officialTitle: Hand Hygiene (Hand Washing)
skills.reasoning: The single universal act. Teaches the before-and-after mindset — nothing happens without it.

skills.officialTitle: Donning and Removing PPE (Gown and Gloves)
skills.reasoning: Builds on hand hygiene: wash before donning and after removing. The sequence is the lesson.

skills.officialTitle: Counts and Records Radial Pulse
skills.reasoning: Simplest vital sign — one finger location, one count, one unit (bpm). Introduces the count-then-record loop.

skills.officialTitle: Counts and Records Respirations
skills.reasoning: Taught immediately after pulse because they share a 60-second window. Observe without alerting the patient.

skills.officialTitle: Measures and Records Manual Blood Pressure
skills.reasoning: Most complex vital sign — equipment, two values, and interpretation. Builds on pulse and respiration rhythm.

skills.officialTitle: Measures and Records Weight of Ambulatory Client
skills.reasoning: Requires the patient to be upright and cooperative. Bridges measurement toward mobility.

skills.officialTitle: Measures and Records Urinary Output
skills.reasoning: The only measurement tied to a bodily output. Bridge skill into the elimination section.

skills.officialTitle: Positions on Side
skills.reasoning: Bed-based, lowest-risk movement. No equipment. Teaches body mechanics, alignment, and patient comfort.

skills.officialTitle: Transfers from Bed to Wheelchair Using Transfer Belt
skills.reasoning: The patient leaves the bed. Requires belt, coordination, and two-point communication.

skills.officialTitle: Assists to Ambulate Using Transfer Belt
skills.reasoning: Most demanding mobility skill — patient fully upright and moving. Gait, balance, and fall prevention all apply.

skills.officialTitle: Performs Modified PROM for One Shoulder
skills.reasoning: Upper body first. Larger joint, more intuitive range of motion. Introduces PROM mechanics.

skills.officialTitle: Performs Modified PROM for One Knee and One Ankle
skills.reasoning: Lower body, two joints. More complex; builds on the shoulder movement pattern already learned.

skills.officialTitle: Applies One Knee-High Elastic Stocking
skills.reasoning: Applied after PROM — supports the same lower-extremity circulation PROM exercises protect. Same goal, different tool.

skills.officialTitle: Gives Modified Bed Bath (Face and One Arm, Hand and Underarm)
skills.reasoning: Largest task — the body itself. Sets the standard for privacy, temperature, and patient dignity.

skills.officialTitle: Provides Mouth Care
skills.reasoning: Moves to the face and head after the bath. Natural clinical sequence — mouth care follows or accompanies bath.

skills.officialTitle: Cleans Upper or Lower Denture
skills.reasoning: Extension of mouth care — same skill with an added device. Follows directly.

skills.officialTitle: Provides Foot Care on One Foot
skills.reasoning: Bottom of the body. Mirrors the lower-extremity focus from PROM. Introduces skin integrity inspection.

skills.officialTitle: Dresses Client with Affected (Weak) Right Arm
skills.reasoning: Last in personal care — requires positioning, affected-side awareness, and sequencing. Most cognitively complex personal care skill.

skills.officialTitle: Feeds Client Who Cannot Feed Self
skills.reasoning: Patient must be clean, dressed, and upright. Mirrors the care shift routine — hygiene before feeding.

skills.officialTitle: Assists With Use of Bedpan
skills.reasoning: Most basic elimination assist — no indwelling device. Teaches privacy, positioning, and cleanup sequence.

skills.officialTitle: Provides Perineal Care (Peri-Care) for Female
skills.reasoning: Post-elimination hygiene step. Completes the loop: assist, clean, document.

skills.officialTitle: Provides Catheter Care for Female
skills.reasoning: Last skill in the entire list. Requires infection control, perineal anatomy, and documentation. Most invasive and highest-consequence skill.
